response: The internet originated in the 1960s with U.S. Department of Defense‑funded research, leading to ARPANET’s first message in 1969 and the adoption of TCP/IP in 1983, which laid the groundwork for today’s network. Tim Berners‑Lee’s invention of the World Wide Web in 1989 (HTML, URLs, HTTP) and the release of the Mosaic browser in 1993 made the web usable by the public, sparking the dot‑com boom of the late 1990s and later the rise of social media, mobile internet after the 2007 iPhone launch, and cloud computing pioneered by AWS in 2006. Currently, over 5 billion people are online, and the internet underpins virtually all facets of modern life, from commerce to communication to entertainment.
